Common Georgia Bugs

The Best Pest Control for Atlanta's Top Pests

Experts in Pest Control for Cockroaches in Georgia

Pests — especially cockroaches — can be a nuisance. That’s why Atlanta homeowners trust Orkin to help manage all cockroach issues, including flying roaches in Georgia. Our methods can help take back your home from these invaders — also known as Georgia palmetto bugs or water bugs.

Seasonal Pest Experts

Seasonal Atlanta Pest Control Solutions

Pests don’t take a break when the weather changes. We believe there are six pest seasons, each requiring a unique approach to year-round protection.

It’s Breeding Season

Breeding season occurs during May and June in Atlanta, Georgia. During this time, common bugs in Georgia are in a frenzy to find their mate. Our pest control service can help protect your home from growing pest populations.

Cockroaches

During the breeding season, female cockroaches in Atlanta attract a mate for reproduction. As they reproduce rapidly, preventing a cockroach infestation in Atlanta is critical; they can cause respiratory problems, contaminate surfaces, and spread bacteria.

Flying Insects

Flying insects in Georgia (mosquitoes and flies) are a nuisance during breeding season. During reproduction, repelling pesky flies in Georgia is critical since they spread diseases. Orkin's Atlanta mosquito control services can help manage flying insects.

June Bugs

During the breeding season, June bugs in Georgia can cause damage to foliage by chewing on leaves and leaving dry, bald patches in lawns. Also known as May bugs, Orkin Pros are trained to help manage these brown beetles in Georgia and similar pests.

Pest Control for Every Season

Seasonal Atlanta Pest Control for Year-Round Protection

Tackle pests — like termites and cockroaches — with year-round pest management

Since pests are present year-round, you need a knowledgeable Atlanta exterminator by your side. Our proactive approach to seasonal pest control addresses patterns in local pest behavior during each season. We use the latest technologies to tackle native rodents, spiders, crawling pests, and flying bugs in Georgia. Seasonal pest control is key to keeping Georgia bugs that bite at bay and under control.

Year-Round Atlanta Termite Control

Year-Round Atlanta Termite Control

Risks

Termites are wood-eating pests that sneak in undetected, create a cozy nest, and gnaw away at your building's structure — potentially causing significant damage. Our personalized approach to Atlanta termite control can help you get rid of termites.

Warning signs

Addressing a termite infestation is critical as these destructive insects can wreak havoc. If you see warning signs such as swarms of flying termites, termite nests, or wood damage, don't wait until the damage has been done. Call Orkin today.

Identification

Termites come in various sizes, colors, and species — including subterranean, dry wood, and damp wood varieties. While most termites look similar, Orkin Pros are expert-trained in bug biology and can correctly identify these tiny black bugs in Georgia.

Inspection

By using state-of-the-art technology, we can track down these destructive, hidden pests. Our experts can conduct a free Atlanta termite inspection at a time that's convenient for you. It's our mission to help you protect your property from these tiny bugs.

Treatment

For termite treatment in Atlanta, you need an expert Atlanta exterminator by your side. Orkin Pros may use a combination of termite treatments — including Sentricon® bait, liquid barriers, or dry foam filler — to help tackle your unique termite situation.

Prevention

We’re here to help you get rid of termites — and help prevent them from coming back. By taking a proactive approach to termites in Atlanta, we can help manage a current infestation while also helping to safeguard your property against future invasions.

Year-Round Control for Atlanta Roaches

Year-Round Control for Atlanta Roaches

Risks

More than just a nuisance, cockroaches may harbor dangerous bacteria, viruses, and other pathogens that might harm humans or household pets. An Orkin exterminator in Atlanta can help keep your loved ones safe all year round.

Warning signs

Some telltale signs of a potential cockroach infestation are visible droppings, oval-shaped eggs in hidden locations, and a strong, unpleasant odor. Whether you witness a cockroach run across the floor or encounter flying roaches in Georgia, we can help.

Inspection

If you suspect cockroach activity in your home, contact Orkin for a free inspection. At a convenient time, an Atlanta exterminator will thoroughly inspect your property — from top to bottom, inside and out — for current or potential roach problems.

Identification

Orkin Pros thoroughly identify areas that various types of roaches in Georgia may infest, including crevices, cracks, and shared spaces like kitchens and bathrooms. Water bugs in Georgia don't stand a chance against Orkin's team of cockroach exterminators.

Treatment

If you’re wondering how to get rid of cockroaches in Georgia, we can help. Our targeted treatment methods — including bait, insecticide sprays, and growth regulators — can help you win the beetle battle against Georgia palmetto bugs.

Prevention

You can help prevent Atlanta roaches from sneaking into your home by sealing gaps and cracks and maintaining a clean environment. Since every property is different, an Orkin Pro can work with you to keep these pesky bugs out of your home.

Frequently Asked Questions

We're taking calls 24-7 for pest control problems in Atlanta. We can help set up an appointment to send out an exterminator near your Atlanta home or business as soon as we confirm your pest service needs.

You can sign up for an appointment or call your local Atlanta pest control branch for a free termite inspection. To get the most accurate cost for a pest control inspection we recommend you call your local pest control branch in Atlanta to confirm availability and have an exterminator exam your property.

Yes, there are several different species of cockroaches that can be found in Atlanta. Cockroaches can carry diseases and are known to fly and crawl around areas to look for food and shelter. "re several bugs that can be found in Atlanta including common pests like ants, bedbugs, bees, centipedes, earwigs, fleas, crickets, moths, millipedes, centipedes, beetles, flies, hornets, mosquitoes, roaches, ticks, spiders, all types of termites, wasps and more.

Yes, rodents can be found in Atlanta such as rats and mice. These pests can carry diseases and crawl and climb into homes or businesses in Atlanta through cracks in the foundation to look for food or shelter. They can chew through walls and electrical wires causing significant damage.

Yes, mosquitoes can be found in Atlanta. Mosquitoes can carry diseases like Zika virus, Chikungunya virus, West Nile virus, Malaria and more. Mosquitoes breed in water, so it is important to remove standing water around your home or business in Atlanta.

Yes, you can find rats in Atlanta. Rats can carry diseases and cause severe damage to homes and businesses in Atlanta. It is important to remove these pests quickly through a professional exterminator and disinfect any surfaces they have touched.

Yes, bees can be found in Atlanta. Bees are usually harmless but will attack if they feel provoked, so it is important to keep your distance.

Atlanta pest control costs can vary depending on the service, frequency of service required, and the size of the area affected. Our standard pest control service in Atlanta covers 15 pests. To get an accurate pest control cost in Atlanta it is recommended to first get an inspection for a quote.

There are several bugs that can be found in Atlanta including common pests like ants, bedbugs, bees, centipedes, earwigs, fleas, crickets, moths, millipedes, centipedes, beetles, flies, hornets, mosquitoes, roaches, ticks, spiders, all types of termites, wasps and more.

Yes, ticks can be found in Atlanta. Most ticks will live on their host (animal or human) and feed on their blood. It is very important to be careful when removing ticks as they can release dangerous fluids.

Yes, there are termites in Atlanta. Termites can cause significant property damage and need to be removed immediately by a professional pest control company.

Yes, bed bugs can be found in Atlanta. Bed bugs tend to live near humans and can hide under furniture, bedding, mattresses, bed frames, carpets, or baseboards. They can travel from place to place on luggage unknowingly and cause an infestation very quickly in your Atlanta home or business.

Yes, you can find several species of ants in Atlanta. Ants can bite and cause damage to homes and businesses, so it is important to remove these pests through a professional exterminator in Atlanta near you.
